cli.help:
OmniGlyph — proksi penjimat token untuk Claude Code

Penggunaan:
  omniglyph                jalankan proksi (tiada flag)
  omniglyph export [...]   render fail/diff ke halaman PNG + laporan kos (lihat omniglyph export --help)

Proksi ini memampatkan tool, skema, peringatan, tool_results,
dan sejarah yang layak; menjejak peristiwa ke cakera; dan mengukur
saved_pct sebenar melalui /v1/messages/count_tokens. Kawalan papan pemuka
boleh melumpuhkan pemampatan secara langsung.

Statistik, sesi, dan alat pembersihan berada di papan pemuka di
  http://127.0.0.1:<port>/  (port lalai 47821)

Flag:
  -h, --help              tunjukkan bantuan ini
      --version           tunjukkan versi

Persekitaran:
  PORT                    port dengar (lalai 47821)
  HOST                    antara muka untuk mengikat (lalai 127.0.0.1, loopback sahaja).
                          Tetapkan 0.0.0.0 untuk mendedahkan papan pemuka di luar host — perhatikan ia
                          tidak disahkan dan menyajikan konteks permintaan yang ditangkap.
  OMNIGLYPH_UPSTREAM         asas API upstream untuk setiap keluarga API
  ANTHROPIC_UPSTREAM      asas API Anthropic; mengatasi OMNIGLYPH_UPSTREAM
                           (lalai https://api.anthropic.com)
  OPENAI_UPSTREAM         asas API OpenAI; mengatasi OMNIGLYPH_UPSTREAM
                           (lalai https://api.openai.com)
  OPENAI_API_KEY          override pilihan kunci OpenAI; jika tidak, diteruskan
  OMNIGLYPH_PROVIDER         pilihan: 'cloudflare-ai-gateway' — hala kedua-dua keluarga API
                          melalui satu URL asas gateway
  OMNIGLYPH_GATEWAY_BASE_URL URL asas gateway (diperlukan dengan OMNIGLYPH_PROVIDER)
  OMNIGLYPH_GATEWAY_HEADERS  header upstream tambahan: objek JSON atau k=v;k2=v2
  OMNIGLYPH_MODELS           asas model dipisah koma untuk dijadikan imej (Claude + GPT);
                          lalai claude-fable-5,gpt-5.6; off melumpuhkan
  OMNIGLYPH_CONFIG           laluan config JSON (lalai ~/.config/OmniGlyph/config.json)
                          menyokong {"models": [...]} atau {"models": "off"}
  OMNIGLYPH_LOG              laluan peristiwa JSONL (lalai ~/.omniglyph/events.jsonl)
  OMNIGLYPH_DUMP_DIR         debug: tulis setiap PNG yang dirender di sini (apa yang model
                          lihat); off melainkan ditetapkan. Hanya lengan mampat.
  OMNIGLYPH_DEBUG_CAPTURE_4XX  debug: tetapkan kepada 1 untuk menyimpan badan permintaan 4xx penuh
                          (prompt + sebarang rahsia dalam konteks) ke cakera. Off secara
                          lalai; telemetri di cakera hanya menyimpan hash.

Gunakan dengan Claude Code:
  ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:47821 claude

Gunakan dengan klien GPT serasi OpenAI:
  OPENAI_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:47821/v1
cli.exportHelp:
omniglyph export — render kod/teks ke halaman PNG untuk konteks LLM yang dimampatkan

Penggunaan:
  omniglyph export [target ...]    sasaran lalai ialah "." (direktori semasa)

Sasaran:
  Fail atau direktori untuk disertakan. Berbilang sasaran digabungkan dengan baris
  pemisah pengepala. Lalai kepada "." apabila tiada diberikan.

Pilihan:
  --include <glob>   sertakan hanya fail yang sepadan dengan glob (boleh diulang)
  --exclude <glob>   kecualikan fail yang sepadan dengan glob (boleh diulang)
  --git              render "git diff HEAD" ditambah fail yang belum dijejak
  --diff <ref>       render "git diff <ref>"
  --stdin            baca teks sumber daripada stdin dan bukan fail
  --out <dir>        direktori asas output (lalai $TMPDIR atau /tmp)
  --model <id>       id model untuk anggaran token visi (lalai claude-sonnet-4-5)
  --json             cetak laporan sebagai JSON
  --open             dedahkan folder output apabila selesai (macOS) supaya anda boleh
                     seret halaman PNG terus ke dalam sembang anda
  -h, --help         tunjukkan bantuan ini

Output:
  <out>/OmniGlyph-export-<hash>/
    page-001.png ...  halaman imej yang dirender
    factsheet.txt     token ketepatan verbatim (laluan, SHA, id, nombor)
    manifest.json     metadata + laporan token
    prompt.txt        arahan ejen sedia-tampal yang merujuk kepada imej

Lajur laporan:
  text tokens   anggaran token jika sumber dihantar sebagai teks biasa
  image tokens  anggaran token untuk menghantar halaman PNG yang dirender
  % saved       (text − image) / text × 100

Contoh:
  omniglyph export .                              # seluruh direktori
  omniglyph export --include "*.ts" src/          # hanya fail TypeScript
  omniglyph export --git                          # perubahan belum dikomit
  omniglyph export --diff HEAD~3                  # 3 komit terakhir
  omniglyph export --open src/                    # render src/, kemudian dedahkan folder
  cat big-file.txt | omniglyph export --stdin
dash.page.overview.title: Overview
dash.page.overview.sub: Live savings at a glance since this proxy started.
dash.page.flow.title: Live Flow
dash.page.flow.sub: How each request flows through the gate, live.
dash.page.telemetry.title: Telemetry
dash.page.telemetry.sub: Recent requests and what became an image vs stayed text.
dash.page.benchmarks.title: Benchmarks
dash.page.sessions.title: Sessions
dash.page.history.title: History
dash.toggle.hint: kill switch · resets to on when you restart
dash.toggle.passthroughBody: — compression is off. Every request goes to Claude unchanged: no images, no savings. Use this to A/B test, or if the upstream API is having problems.
dash.toggle.confirmOff:
Turn compression off?

Requests will pass straight through to Claude, unchanged. Restarting the proxy turns it back on.
dash.models.hintClaude: everything else is sent as normal text · runtime only · persist with OMNIGLYPH_MODELS
dash.models.hintGpt: imaging only, no Anthropic cache_control · one scope for all families · set OMNIGLYPH_MODELS (CSV of bases, or off) to persist
dash.models.moot: compression is off, so this has no effect right now
dash.hero.subEmpty: Point Claude Code at this proxy and send a message. The moment a request flows through, your running savings show up right here.
dash.hero.headline: {word} after caching
dash.hero.sub: {actual} effective tokens vs {baseline} if this same context stayed plain text — both counted after normal cache discounts since this proxy started. Your latest messages and Claude’s live output are never compressed.
dash.hero.meta: Cache-aware — cached reads counted at their real ~0.1× weight, not full price · output untouched ({output}) · no $ assumptions
dash.feed.title: Live feed
dash.timeline.title: Timeline
dash.flow.sectionSub: how each request flows through the gate
dash.flow.ariaLabel: Request pipeline: client to gate to renderer or passthrough to Anthropic API to response
dash.flow.ribbonWaiting: Waiting for the first request — point Claude Code at this proxy to see it flow.
dash.flow.ribbonPos: {pct} fewer weighted input tokens · {tok} tok · {usd} saved
dash.flow.ribbonNeg: {pct} more weighted input tokens than plain text so far — the gate keeps unprofitable requests as passthrough
dash.drawer.intro: Every number above, derived from the same per-event log. The proxy only moves input tokens; output is shown on both sides so percentages stay honest.
dash.drawer.splitWhy: partition the paid-rows set by which path actually ran (compressed vs passthrough). Same $/Mtok on both sides so the rate assumption cancels in the delta. Selection bias (the gate routes each turn) does NOT cancel — read with the sample counts.
dash.drawer.pctDiagnostic: this is a counterfactual ("what you WOULD have paid"). It leans on the count_tokens probe, the cache-aware split, and an input-rate assumption. Useful as a sanity check; the real-traffic answer is the compressed-vs-passthrough split above.
dash.ctxmap.sectionSub: click a request to see image vs text
dash.ctxmap.evictedNote: That request's breakdown isn't kept anymore — only the most recent requests are. Pick {details} on a newer row.
dash.ctxmap.emptyNote: Pick {details} on a request to see exactly which parts became images and which stayed as text.
dash.ctxmap.splitNoteImg: OmniGlyph can misread exact values inside images — treat these as gist, not byte-exact.
dash.ctxmap.splitNoteTxt: never imaged — safe for IDs, hashes and exact numbers.
dash.ctxmap.pagesTitle: {n} image {page} sent to Claude — click one to read the exact text behind it:
dash.ctxmap.pagesTitleRestored: {n} image {page} were sent — thumbnails expired when the proxy restarted. The breakdown above is reconstructed from the saved log.
dash.recent.colSentAsTitle: Was this request’s context compressed into an image?
dash.recent.colAsTextTitle: Billing-equivalent input if kept as plain text, after cache create/read rates
dash.recent.colSentTitle: Actual billing-equivalent input after imaging, after cache create/read rates
dash.recent.colSavedLostTitle: As-text minus Sent; negative means imaging cost more
dash.recent.empty: No requests yet — they stream in here live.
dash.recent.createNoteTitle: Cache-create turn: this loss is the one-time {rate}× premium for writing {cc} tokens to cache. Later turns re-read that prefix at {readRate}×, which typically recoups it.
dash.sessions.axis: tokens saved per session (cache-aware) · top {shown} of {total}
dash.bench.sectionSub: billing-sweep + density-frontier — real receipts, live runs gated
dash.bench.harnessUnavailable: Running these harnesses requires a full repository checkout — the benchmarks/ scripts aren’t part of the npm package. Clone the repo and run them from the CLI instead.
dash.bench.densitySplitNote: one row per (model · config) experiment — densities differ by design; the production-config numbers are the ones the gate enforces.
dash.bench.confirmLive: This calls the real provider API and can incur real cost. Results append to benchmarks/{harness}/results/. Continue?
dash.bench.liveTitle: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set in this process — required for a live run
dash.viewer.evicted: image #{n} is no longer in the buffer
dash.viewer.empty: No images yet — they appear the instant OmniGlyph compresses a request.
dash.viewer.showSource: show the text behind this image
dash.viewer.noSource: source text wasn’t captured for this image
dash.overview.lifetimeSub: the full math, and the honesty receipts behind it
dash.strip.costTip: Average real cost of a request with imaging on vs off (passthrough), measured on your own traffic.
dash.strip.costCollectingTip: Needs at least {n} paid requests on each path before the comparison is trustworthy.
dash.strip.savedTokensSub: vs sending the same context as text
dash.strip.savedTokensTip: Bulky context (system prompt, tool output, old turns) sent as compact images instead of text. Cache-aware, input side only — recent turns and the live output stay text.
dash.strip.estSavedTip: A rough dollar figure: saved tokens × the input price. Actual savings depend on your plan and caching — see the math drawer.
dash.ctxmap.headlineSmaller: {pctSpan} smaller — {textNoun} would bill as {base} input tokens; images billed as {real}
dash.ctxmap.headlineBigger: {pctSpan} bigger — images billed as {real} input tokens vs {base} for {textNoun}
dash.ctxmap.subnoteNoBaseline: Billed tokens count cache discounts (reads at 0.1×) — no trustworthy text baseline for this request yet.
dash.ctxmap.subnoteCold: No warm text cache this turn — the text counterfactual’s prefix is priced at the 1.25× create rate (the same event the imaged path pays), identical basis to the Saved column. The gap is purely token count. {rawPhrase}
dash.ctxmap.subnoteWarmLossCheapRead: Billed = after cache discounts (reads at 0.1×), same basis as the Saved column. The raw text is {rawShrink}% smaller, but most of it would have been a cheap cache-read — so imaging it cost more.
dash.ctxmap.subnoteWarmDefault: Billed = after cache discounts (reads at 0.1×), same basis as the Saved column. {rawPhrase}
dash.ctxmap.pageTitle: Click to read the source text behind page {n}
